review_text:
Food was delicious. However, my boyfriend and I are both allergic to nuts. We got takeout and realized we forgot to say something about our allergy. We called back and asked if either of the dishes we ordered had nuts in them. Some guy on the phone curtly said “no nuts on the whole menu” and promptly hung up. review_text:
First off, the atmosphere is totally authentic and inviting. The place looks great coming off a fresh remodel. The host and wait staff are kind and attentive. The menu was an awesome selection of authentic Mexican cuisine. Also, the drink menu is a must if you're looking to kick back and relax after a long day. Me and my wife were in awe of the food presentation. Honestly, though, only a small part of the experience. The flavors and taste definitely were all there. My wife ordered the chicken burritos, and I ordered the three pork tacos meal. It being taco Tuesday. I just had to! The portions are large, and we ended up taking home for our lunches the next day. It's the restaurant's 25th anniversary. Being in business that long is not shocking. They are definitely killing it when it comes to authentic Mexican cuisine. Do yourself and taste buds a favor. Stop in and give them a try. You won't be disappointed. They also have a long list of different tequilas.

Update: Took a coworker to lunch. Man oh man we weren't disappointed. So delicious and we were happy to try a new menu addition! I usually don’t leave reviews, but the experience I had tonight was so unbearable, I wouldn’t be doing my due diligence in helping others save their money.

My wife ordered our family’s dinner and I planned on picking it up on my way home from work. When I arrived at the location, I waited 5 minutes to be greeted, there was no one in sight and when any of the employees would see me, they would just stare and keeping doing what they were doing.

When I was finally greeted, the waitress was nice “initially” and apologized for the wait. As she was handing me my food, she asked me was the meals paid for and I said yes. As I was ready to drive off, she comes out yelling stating I didn’t pay for the food. I told her my wife made the order and that I thought everything was taken care of on the phone. She angrily said “we don’t take payments over the phone!” As a worker of the restaurant, it’s your responsibility to check what the status of the meals are and what’s paid/not paid. How am I suppose to know your procedure? This is only my second time at this location.

So I told her I thought the payment was taken care of and it was no problem; I will come in and pay (it wasn’t a big deal). As I went back into the restaurant I was greeted by another employee named Tatyana (she was great and really helpful throughout the entire situation). When I began to pay for my food, I was told that the employee who caught my attention outside kept saying “I got him!”, which alluded that she actually thought that I was actually trying to steal even though I told her multiple times about the situation.

Fast forward, my family is eating and my son wanted chicken tenders and fries. As he was eating his chicken tenders he found a long piece of hair mid bite on his chicken, mind you he’s only 4 years old. I called the restaurant and talked to Tatyana again and she made sure he received a different order (which was the Mac & Cheese). After getting off the phone with Tatyana, my wife alerted me that she had hair in her rice. Now at this point I am angry and disappointed, this was a waste of my hard earned money.

When I went to pick up my son’s food, I told an employee what transpired with my wife’s food as well. At this point my wife was so disgusted she didn’t want any replacement for her food. I asked the employee if the chefs wear any hairnets or use gloves while preparing the food and I was told no. When I returned to my hotel to give my son his remade food, he showed me he had a long piece of hair in his food. We immediately threw all the food away.

I know this was long winded, but this was one of the worst experiences at a restaurant I’ve ever had and we will never return to this location. Beware ordering food from here because I was told there’s new management, save your money.

I dont write reviews unless there was something extremely good or very bad. This was bad. I live in the area. We stopped coming here because of the food and service years ago. They remodeled and its so much better now. The last time we were here a couple months ago, the serving sizes were small but pretty good. This time was very bad. There were 5 of us, we all sent our plates back.

The food we waited 35+ minutes for wasn't much warmer than room temp. How can the kitchen be slow and serve cold food? Saturday night should have your A-team kitchen staff. The sad, flat burritos were small and had little flavor. Who plates an already small burrito on a platter size plate with a puddle of liquid beans and tower of rice? $16, never again. First year culinary student mistakes there. I've had better/ more food from a food truck in half the time. There is another local place you’ll wait 60-90 minutes for a table and the burritos are $20+ but its so big you can get 3 meals out of it.

After 5 people sending the food back, we should have left and not eaten the replacement food, one of us didn't and sent it back again. We didn't pay for that one, she's the smart one. The saving grace was the server being nice and immediately called the manager. He was fast to come over but did not look like a manager with his regular jacket on. He was nice and 5 "new" plates of food came out in about 6-8 minutes. How does that work with a super slow kitchen?Management sets the tone of how a place is run. His appearance was pretty telling.

I'm genuinely concerned what my body is going to do over the the next 12-24 hours after this. Wish me luck.

review_text:
I ordered the beef enchiladas dinner. When it arrived, it looked delicious, but after taking a bite of one enchilada, I immediately noticed something was off. I thought maybe it had cooled on the edge of the plate, so I tried the middle one, same result. The meat tasted old and rancid.

I rarely send food back because I worry about retaliation from the kitchen, but my friend insisted I say something. I called the waiter over and explained that I didn’t like the taste of the enchiladas and asked if I could order something else. He seemed confused and called the manager over.

The manager asked if the dish was what I ordered. I confirmed that it was, but explained again that the meat tasted rancid. He took the plate away, and the waiter took my replacement order: shrimp, chicken, and steak fajitas.

When the fajitas arrived, they were completely covered in cheese, and all the proteins were mixed together instead of separated. It honestly looked like leftovers thrown onto a plate and smothered in cheese. I tried a few bites of shrimp and steak but had to stop…it was a mess. No one came back to check whether the replacement dish was satisfactory.

Overall, the food was bad and the service was worse. In hindsight, I should have taken the bland, watered-down salsa as a warning that this “Mexican” restaurant has been toned down for the community. I won’t be returning.
owner_response: Thank you for sharing your experience, Kimberly. We appreciate your feedback and are sorry to hear that the dishes did not meet your expectations. For dessert I had a cheesecake chimichanga and it was delivered with the cutest little edible drawing. The perfect end to a perfect night. This place really does go out of their way to make your experience one you will positively remember.

This restaurant offers take out, dine in(indoor and outdoor seating), and delivery(though partners like Grubhub) meal options.

Please support this establishment if you’re thinking about dropping in. Everyone is wonderful and this is definitely a little restaurant you can call home that will have you excited to come back each time. 🫶

(The chiles rellenos ordered are a trio with extra cheese from the Al la carte menu paired with a side of steak for anyone looking to order! The steak is cooked perfectly and I personally layer it on top of the dish.)

review_text: Food was bland, service was very slow.  The design and atmosphere was the best part. They have a robot bring food to your table. The highlights.....

review_text: We had a reservation for a memorial luncheon.  The service was so slow and the food was cold.  We sat there for 3 hours.  The waiter hardly checked on us.

This is my go-to Mexican restaurant. Last night I was craving Mexican and my husband wasn't, so he picked up my carry out order and got McDonald's for himself. His cheeseburger meal was actually more expensive than my dinner! I had a huge burrito, quesadilla, tostado and a side of chips and salsa for less than $15 and it was really tasty. You can't beat that.

Was better years ago. Now, they get the order wrong often and the cost is high enough that it can’t be forgiven. We thought it might be an issue just with takeout, but our dine in experience was not better. Good luck if you want cheese on your chorizo quesadilla. The best item has been the steak tacos American style. Unfortunately, we won’t be back.
Food wasn't as good this time.

Great service! They have a little robot as a food runner, how cool! Quesabirria tacos were delightful, and both of my friend's highly enjoyed their chimichanga and enchilada entrees. Pina Colada and Paloma were on point. I wish I had a Mexican restaurant like this where I'm from!
The food was delicious and well worth the wait. The service took a longer and definitely didn't have enough servers for a Saturday lunch time.

I have been here twice. Both times I have tried very authentic mexican recipes,  each and every time they did not use the authentic types of cheese that you would use in a very authentic Mexican recipe. They use nacho cheese!  Nacho cheese! In place of the for real melty, authentic muenster cheese that you NORMALLY put in regular mexican recipes. Tacos queso birria.. nacho cheese in that.... Yuck!! Put real cheese in that.  That's lazy cooking. Chile rellenos.... tried that too! also tried to pass that off as authentic with some nacho cheese filling. How can you put that on a plate
?! Oh! I know! Money talks! Real Mexican heritage cooking, well, it doesn't matter here. They are very lazy in their cooking. They know better than to use what they're using in mexican cooking.
Good food , will visit again.

Somebody Give Mary a Raise! 🙌🏽🔥

Man, let me tell you somethin’—we pulled up to this Mexican spot just tryna grab some good eats and chill. But what we got? A whole experience. And that’s all thanks to one queen named Mary. I don’t even be writin’ reviews like that, but sis went above and beyond, and I had to show her some love.

Soon as we walked in, Mary greeted us like we was cousins she hadn’t seen in a minute. Big smile, warm energy, like we was already family. It wasn’t no fake waitress vibe either—this was real, straight from the heart. You could feel it. She made sure we was comfortable, took her time walkin’ us through the menu, and even checked on food allergies and spice levels like she already knew what was up. That’s care, not just service. We will be back!
Took the family out to los tres and had a great experience. Marilyn is fast, efficient; friendly and good at her job. We will be back!

We had lunch here and it was delicious!!!! Marilyn was so helpful with her suggestions on the food! My husband has new favorite tacos! He decided to try her favorite tacos and was not disappointed. He said the flavors and the hot sauce in the Al pastor tacos were off the charts!!! Our drinks were on point and never went empty even the kids sodas. Just a wonderful experience all around, for our first time dining here! We will definitely be back!
Always have the best service here. The food never disappoints. Always worth the drive out here.

Marilyn was absolutely amazing from the moment we walked into the restaurant until we walked out the door... The food was great too!
Good food and excellent service.  I would recommend Santiago as a very good waiter.

Marilyn was absolutely wonderful.  10 out of 10 stars. The food was incredible.  I will definitely be back. The service was on point. She was so prompt and helpful.  Period. Wonderful experience.

great food.
Everything was great except for the “new” margarita recipe. Please go back to the old recipe. The new one sucks and we won’t be ordering it again.

Extremely excited that they have a Vegan section on the menu! Way to go! However, the hostess had no idea what Vegan meant when I asked her about the Vegan menu, which was kind of frustrating. Our table was dirty when they sat us down and didn't come back around for a while to clean it. Our second chips and salsa helping was missing the salsa, and we waited for quite some time for the salsa. When the robot came around, the waitress handed me the chicken tacos and said "Vegan Tacos"... Again, not sure why they don't know the difference between chicken and VEGAN CHORIZO. I was a bit confused. My side of black beans was maybe 1/3 of the way filled up, which was also disappointing. But the food was pretty good and like I said, very glad they offer Vegan options. I'll definitely come back and give them a second chance.
Food is great! They are short staffed, so I waiting to get my order taken about 10+mins then the order took longer than 20mins to make. I hope they get more team members for better experiences.

I have been here four times over the past couple of months.

The first time, my partner found mold on her corn tortilla. To their credit, management handled the situation exceptionally well. They took it seriously, apologized, and made it right, which is why I decided to give them another chance.

The next two visits were excellent. The food was great, and the staff was friendly and attentive.

Unfortunately, my fourth visit was frustrating from start to finish.

I first called to place a carryout order, but it seemed like the hostess who answered the phone set it down and forgot about me. I understand they were probably busy, so I simply ordered through the website instead. Since the website doesn't let you specify tortilla type, I called back to confirm that along with my pickup time.

I arrived at the pickup time they gave me, waited nearly ten minutes, and then my order came out missing one of my three meals.

When I pointed it out, the hostess insisted I had only ordered two meals and pointed to her ticket.  She repeatedly insisted I had only ordered two meals until I pulled out my phone and showed her the order confirmation with three meals. Once she realized the mistake, she called the manager over, who told me it would be another 7 to 10 minutes to make the missing meal.

I explained that I had already waited past my pickup time and asked if I could simply receive a refund for the missing meal instead. There was no apology, they wouldn't refund it, and there was no real concern that they had made a mistake. The only solution offered was for me to wait longer while they remade it. Both the manager and the hostess just seemed irritated that there was a problem rather than interested in resolving it.

Eventually, I told them I would just try to sort it out with my credit card company and left.

In the end, I paid for three meals, left with only two, and AGAIN we found MOLD on the tortillas!
